titleStmt.title: Memorandum by Mr. Robert M. McClintock to the Acting Secretary of State

head.hi: Memorandum by Mr. Robert M. McClintock to the Acting Secretary of State

div.p:
In light of this afternoon’s press reports of Shertok’s statement before Committee I of the General Assembly,Mr. Shertok, in a statement before the Committee on November 15, announced that “The Mediator’s proposal concerning the Negeb was in itself a sufficient reason why the Government of Israel could not consider the [Bernadotte] report even a basis for discussion.” He stated also that Israel would not give up any part of the Negeb, that it claimed permanent inclusion in Israel of modern Jewish Jerusalem and of the territory linking Jerusalem with the coast and that all of the Galilee should be part of Israel. The text of Mr. Shertok’s statement is printed in GA, 3rd sess., Pt. I, First Committee, Summary Records, 1948, p. 640.
Ahmad Shukairy, Vice President of the delegation of the Arab Higher Committee at the United Nations, the following day, also rejected Count Bernadotte’s proposals as a basis for settling the Palestine problem (ibid., p. 647). Mr. Satterthwaite and I agree that it might be useful to repeat to the President Ambassador Douglas’ Niact 4851, November 12 attached. Both the press reports and this telegram relate to your description of the President’s feeling
that if he was to keep his contract the Israeli would have to keep theirs.Marginal notation by Mr. Lovett: “Discussed substance with Clifford 5:45 Nov. 15th. No need to send.”
